blocks.text: DTC P0843: Driven pulley oil pressure sensor Failure or sensor circuit disconnection

The driven pulley oil pressure sensor monitors changes in driven pulley control pressure (oil pressure). The PGM-FI ECU supplies a 5 V voltage to the oil pressure sensor through a pull-up register. When the driven pulley oil pressure is low, the electrical resistance of the oil pressure sensor decreases and the PGM-FI ECU detects a low voltage; when the driven pulley oil pressure is high, the electrical resistance of the oil pressure sensor increases and the PGM-FI ECU detects a high voltage. If the driven pulley oil pressure sensor output voltage exceeds the specified value, the PGM-FI ECU determines that a failure has occurred and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off, D indicator light: flashing
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: Driven pulley oil pressure sensor output voltage [Driven pulley oil pressure sensor (V)] exceeds 4.84V for more than 10 seconds.

items.text: Disconnection of the cord (PDN line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the driven pulley oil pressure sensor (including disconnected or loose coupler)
Disconnection of the cord (VCC line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the driven pulley oil pressure sensor (including disconnected or loose coupler)
Disconnection of the cord (SG line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the driven pulley oil pressure sensor (including disconnected or loose coupler)
Driven Pulley oil pressure sensor failure
Turn the select lever to the P position and wait for at least 10 seconds.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the D indicator light will flash without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the DTC and freeze data will be stored.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
